subject:
MCore Error due to: System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could not
 find a part of the path '...m/refinement_temp/c_elegans/worker0'.
body:
Hey gang, 
first thing - WARPTools is awesome and helping me a lot.

I'm trying out M now, and have come across some strange behaviour.

On a previous dataset, it worked absolutely fine, no issues.

However, on my current dataset I'm having issues running: 

MCore \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--iter 0

See the commands and the error message below.

A few things that might be noteworthy:

1. I'm using dev version 29.
2. I'm extracting subtomos in 3D and refining in rln4 (this worked fine on 
other dataset)
3. I have 5 data sources which are arranged in directories like so: 
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_1/
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_2/
    ./warp_tiltseries/group_3/
    etc.

As per the error, the m/refinement_temp/c_elegans is already empty.

Strangely too, the resolution of the map was 10 A or so prior to M
and M reports a resolution of 38.24 here after refining. 

Any ideas on what's going on? I feel a bit stooopid as I can't quite get 
what's going wrong.

Thanks!
Euan

P.S. I tried to revert back to the pre-M state by copying the original .xml 
files
from versions and placing them back in the warp_tiltseries directory
and then deleting the .source files and the M directory. Hopefully this 
didn't mess things up.
I re-extracted particles and ran relion_reconstruct as a sanity check and 
they look fine 

====================================
COMMANDS

MTools create_population \
--directory m \
--name c_elegans

MTools create_source \
--name c_elegans \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--processing_settings group_5_tiltseries.settings

#Repeat for groups 1 to 4.

MTools create_species \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--name ribosome \
--diameter 360 \
--temporal_samples 1 \
--half1 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
--half2 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
--mask relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job039/mask.mrc \
--particles_relion relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_data.star \
--lowpass 11 \
--angpix_resample 2.39

MCore \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--iter 0

====================================
ERROR
...
...
...
Commiting changes in c_elegans...Done
Saving intermediate refinement results for c_elegans...Done
Preparing population for data source c_elegans...Done
Loading gain reference for c_elegans... Done
Refining all series in data source...
2/2                                                                         
  
Commiting changes in c_elegans...Done
Saving intermediate refinement results for c_elegans...Done
Finishing refinement
Gathering intermediate results, then reconstructing and filtering...
ribosome: 38.24 �  

1/1Unhandled exception. System.Exception: Something went wrong during 
refinement. Sorry! Here are the details:

System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could not find a part of the path 
'/g/mattei/euan/Krios/20240926_C_Elegans_LO/m/refinement_temp/c_elegans/worker0'.
   at System.IO.FileSystem.RemoveEmptyDirectory(String fullPath, Boolean 
topLevel, Boolean throwWhenNotEmpty)
   at System.IO.Directory.Delete(String path, Boolean recursive)
   at MCore.MCore.DoProcessing()
   at MCore.MCore.DoProcessing()
   at MCore.MCore.Main(String[] args)
   at MCore.MCore.<Main>(String[] args)
run.sh: line 44: 762754 Aborted                 (core dumped) MCore 
--population m/c_elegans.population --iter 0

====================================
